items.title: French mayor secreted away under threat by notorious DZ mafia drug gang
items.description: Here in France, authorities are waging an increasingly intense battle against rising drug crime. That fight took a new turn this week – as the mayor of the southern town of Alès was spirited from his home by police in the middle of the night over fears for his safety. He'd received multiple threats from the drug gang known as the “DZ Mafia”. Once a local player on the Marseille drug market, the gang has expanded to become one of France's top security threats. Details by Bryan Quinn and our colleagues at France Televisions.

items.title: D. Rykner says the Louvre needs to be 'refurbished', Macron's big project is 'useless and expensive'
items.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Didier Rykner, Art Historian, Investigative Journalist and Founder / Editor in Chief of La Tribune de l’Art. Offering a forceful critique of the Louvre's priorities, Rykner argues that the museum's challenge is not a lack of resources, rather a misallocation of them. While acknowledging that security is a both necessary and inevitable expense, he contends that the greater threat to the Louvre lies in the deterioration of its historic fabric and the diversion of funding towards what he regards as an unnecessary, politically driven architectural expansion.

items.title: Germany greenlights controversial joint French-Russian nuclear project

items.title: 'Our cultural institutions not safe: If they could break into the Louvre, they can get in anywhere'
items.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Christopher Marinello, veteran lawyer , founder of Art Recovery International and one of the world's foremost experts in recovering stolen, looted, and missing works of art. The reopening of the Apollo Gallery at the Louvre, nine months after one of the most brazen museum thefts in recent history, marks more than the restoration of a famous exhibition space. It exposes a broader tension between public access to cultural heritage and the growing vulnerability of museums in an era of increasingly sophisticated organised crime.

items.description: University of Buenos Aires researchers say education investment has almost halved in two years since President Javier Milei took office, while second Grupo EPC analysis says funding for science has fallen by more than 46%. Leer más

items.title: Queensland mayors take disaster funding fight to Melbourne
items.description: Mayors from parts of Queensland hit hard by floods and cyclones have protested in a Melbourne park against planned changes to federal disaster funding.

items.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
items.description: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

items.title: ‘Our bodies contain poison’: Landmark trial in Argentina probes dangers of agrochemicals
items.description: Trial over agrochemical spraying in the city of Pergamino has set an unprecedented legal benchmark: for the first time, a federal court placed the entire agricultural production chain in the dock, from farmers and agronomists to public officials. Trial ends in acquittal for nearly all those excused, except two municipal employees. Leer más

items.title: Qld government denies blocking report into response to domestic violence
items.description: The report was particularly critical of police responses to domestic and family violence incidents, and highlighted a system "marked by fragmentation, inconsistency, and significant procedural and cultural barriers".

items.title: Ghana court jails opposition party's regional chairman for illegal mining
items.description: A Ghanaian High Court has sentenced Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, to 20 years in prison with hard labour for illegal mining.

items.title: Skiing enthusiasts flock to slopes on Lesotho's Maluti Mountain
items.description: Afriski says about 70 per cent of visitors come from neighbouring South Africa while the rest are mostly from across the region.

items.title: DRC's opposition cancel protest after president announces national dialogue
items.description: Opposition parties in the Democratic Republic of the Congo are postponing a protest after President Felix Tshisekedi announced the launch of a national dialogue.

items.title: Rubio says United States wants to globalise fight against far-left 'terrorism'
items.description: Argentina among nations represented at meeting on "The Resurgence of Political Terrorism" hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Leer más

items.title: France passes social media ban for teenagers under 15
items.description: The French parliament has approved a social media ban for children under the age of 15. The measure is due to come into effect after the Summer holidays – but many questions still need to be answered as to how it will be enforced. Story by James Vasina.

items.title: Hasta el final: What propels Argentina's comeback kings
items.description: A string of dramatic comebacks and extra-time victories fuels confidence that the Albiceleste will fight until the very end – and succeed. Leer más

items.title: Iranian official meets mediators in Pakistan as Iran and US keep up 10th day of attacks
items.description: The visit by Iran’s Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni comes as Pakistan has intensified diplomatic efforts in recent days to resuscitate the deal

items.title: Pampa approves US$2.7-billion Argentina urea plant amid shale boom
items.description: First new facility of this kind in almost quarter century; Plant to be built over three years in Argentina’s petrochemicals hub in Bahía Blanca Leer más

items.title: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye elected new Chairman of ECOWAS
items.description: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye is the new Chairman of ECOWAS. The 46-year-old was elected during the regional bloc's summit in Sierra Leone, taking over from President Julius Maada Bio.

items.title: Female coaches want more representation in African football
items.description: Female head coaches will be a rare sight at the Women’s African Cup of Nations. However, there are some promising signs.

items.title: Beluga whales relocated from closed Canadian marine park arrive in US
items.description: Six beluga whales have arrived in the US after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.

items.title: US to spend $5 billion on health, construction research powered by AI
Argentina's press laments Albiceleste defeat, hails end of an era
items.description: Press reflects on Messi's emotional farewell and an uncertain future; Headlines focus on tears, farewell fears and the end of an era. Leer más

items.title: Spain shatters Argentina's dream with 1-0 win in World Cup final
items.description: Disappointment for Argentina and skipper Lionel Messi in World Cup final; Barcelona striker Ferran Torres lashes home a thumping finish in the 106th minute to reward a dominant display and win Spain the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni on final defeat: Spain deserved to win World Cup
items.description: Argentina coach casts doubt over his future after World Cup final loss, says his team must accept defeat with the same grace they showed when celebrating recent successes. Leer más

items.title: TE Connectivity projects upbeat quarterly results, to buy Astrodyne for $1.4 billion
China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
items.description: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country's last primary steelmaking facility. China's Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
items.description: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

items.title: England and Liverpool football legend Kevin Keegan dies aged 75
items.description: English former striker and manager Kevin Keegan has passed away at the age of 75 after a battle with cancer. He was a two-time European footballer of the year with Hamburg.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit more sites of Russian online retailer Wildberries
items.description: Logistics hubs belonging to Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions were struck overnight.

items.title: Consumer prices trump geopolitics
items.description: Like its counterparts in most other countries, the US electorate is more interested in consumer goods than in geopolitical questions that, sooner or later, could have devastating consequences. Leer más

items.title: Watch Texas police officer pull man from burning car
items.description: Bodycam and dashcam footage shows a police officer rescue a man seconds before his car was engulfed in flames in Texas.

items.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect
items.description: Under new rules to reduce waste, large companies across the European Union can no longer dispose of unsold clothes, accessories and footwear.

items.title: Group of Indigenous Taiwanese row over 100 miles to recreate their ancestors' journey
items.description: Indigenous people in Taiwan complete a journey to the Philippines in a traditional rowboat, recreating a journey their ancestors made thousands of years ago.

items.title: What's holding up the Gaza stabilization mission?
items.description: A multinational stabilization mission is supposed to ensure the reconstruction of Gaza. Without political consensus among Israel, Hamas and the Arab states, its implementation remains uncertain.

items.title: Mounting violence in Mali poses threat to military-led governement
items.description: Mali’s military has suffered one of its deadliest losses in years. At least 50 soldiers were killed in an ambush near the northern town of Anefis, in an attack claimed by Tuareg separatists and Al-Qaeda-linked jihadists. The assault is the latest in a string of setbacks, with militants also targeting Malian and Russian Africa Corps forces, downing a military helicopter, and briefly seizing territory. More with Hénio Nsaibia, West Africa Senior Analyst at ACLED.

items.title: Rebel Wilson cleared in defamation case brought by actress in her own film
items.description: Wilson was accused of defaming young Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes, but cleared by a Sydney court.

items.title: $16 million mansion set for demolition catches fire twice in a night
items.description: Fire broke out twice in one night at a $16-million mansion on the NSW Mid North Coast. It was approved for demolition just weeks ago, and police are treating the blazes as suspicious.

items.title: Daily Quiz | Notable events of July 22 in history
items.description: Try to test your knowledge on some very notable events of this day in history

items.title: Morocco prepares for 2030 FIFA World Cup with major hotel expansion
items.description: For the first time in its history, Morocco will co-host the tournament with Spain and Portugal. To make sure the event runs smoothly, the country plans to add 60,000 hotel beds by 2030, according to the tourism minister.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 10 to 17
items.description: A selection of stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Strike on Ukraine-bound gas tanker in Black Sea wounds three
items.description: The Black Sea has been a regular theatre of confrontation between Ukraine and Russia since the Kremlin's full-scale invasion began in 2022

items.title: CREDICORP launches C.L.I.C.K.D. Scheme with FG’s 3MTT Programme
items.description: The programme provides for expansion of affordable consumer credit for locally-assembled laptops and devices for digital workers The post CREDICORP launches C.L.I.C.K.D. Scheme with FG’s 3MTT Programme appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .
